pattern: Build a reversed-string trie over wordsContainer, storing at each node the index of the shortest (then earliest) container word reachable from that node, then answer each query by traversing the trie on the reversed query.
hints: Common suffix length between two strings equals the length of the longest common prefix of their reversals. Reverse all strings and work with prefixes.

Insert all reversed container strings into a trie. At each trie node record the best container index: smallest length, tie-broken by earliest index. Propagate this upward so every prefix node already holds the answer for any query whose reversed form passes through it.
For each reversed query string, walk the trie as far as possible. The node you last reached holds the best matching container index.

edge_inputs.description: single container word, every query returns 0
all container words identical, pick index 0
query has no common suffix with any container, return shortest container
tie on suffix length, pick shortest container; further tie goes to earliest index
query is longer than any container but shares full container as suffix
container strings that are suffixes of each other; shortest wins

stress.description: moderate random string sets to verify trie against brute force
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    nc = rng.randint(1, size)
    nq = rng.randint(1, size)
    container = [''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 10))) for _ in range(nc)]
    query = [''.join(rng.choice('abcde') for _ in range(rng.randint(1, 10))) for _ in range(nq)]
    return [container, query]
